title: Deploy to GitHub Pages
description: The app is fully static (index.html + src/), no build step. Publish it to GitHub Pages so there's a live URL. README already lists this as a next step.
design: Add a GitHub Actions Pages workflow (or configure Pages to serve from the default branch root). Note src/ paths are relative so root serving works. Confirm swisstopo tiles load from the Pages origin (README notes sandboxed embeds can block them; a real origin should be fine). CDN Leaflet + external tiles need no bundling.
acceptance_criteria: Pushing to the default branch publishes a working live URL where the map, routing, and GPX export function; swisstopo tiles load (no gray).
close_reason: Added .github/workflows/pages.yml: assembles index.html+src into _site and deploys via GitHub Pages Actions on push to master. Needs one-time repo setting: Pages → Source → GitHub Actions.

title: Batch consecutive routed legs into one BRouter call with straight= indices
description: recompute() (app.js:87-104) issues one BRouter request per routed leg. A route with many waypoints fires many sequential requests. Batch consecutive routed waypoints into a single BRouter call, passing the direct-leg positions via BRouter's straight= index parameter, cutting requests and latency.
design: BRouter accepts multiple lonlats plus straight=<comma-separated indices> to force straight segments between given via points. Group the waypoint chain into runs, send one request per run with all vias, marking direct legs as straight indices. Reconcile with legKey/legCache (currently per-leg pair) — cache key becomes the run signature. Preserve per-leg mode toggling and the failed-leg dashed-red fallback (app.js:98-102).
acceptance_criteria: A route of N routed waypoints issues far fewer than N BRouter requests; drawn path and stats match the per-leg version; direct legs still render straight and never hit the router; a failing run degrades gracefully.
notes: Larger refactor than the hover features; touches caching + recompute control flow.
close_reason: Reframed from single-call batching to concurrent per-leg fetches. True straight= batching returns one merged track and would regress the per-leg model (hover highlight, Routed/Direct toggle, dashed-direct styling, error fallback, DEM elevation). Instead recompute now fires all routed-leg BRouter requests concurrently (were sequential), preserving one polyline per leg and all per-leg features. Big wall-clock win on multi-leg routes; same request count.

title: Elevation for direct legs via swissAlti3D DEM sampling
description: Direct legs currently carry no elevation, so total ascent (statAsc) is summed from routed legs only — the '*ascent from routed legs only' caveat in index.html. Sample the swisstopo swissAlti3D DEM height service along direct-leg points to give them real elevation and fold that ascent into the total.
design: Direct legs are built in recompute() (app.js:89-91) as [[a.lat,a.lng],[b.lat,b.lng]] with dist via haversine and no ascend. Query swisstopo height API (api3.geo.admin.ch height service, LV95 coords) at sampled points along each direct segment, compute positive ascent, add to asc. Cache per-leg like legCache. Coords need WGS84->LV95 conversion for the height endpoint (see the EPSG:2056/proj4 issue). Update the index.html footnote once direct legs carry elevation.
acceptance_criteria: Direct legs contribute positive ascent to the m-ascent stat; results cached so dragging/toggling doesn't refetch unchanged legs; graceful fallback (0, no crash) if the height service is unreachable.
close_reason: Direct legs now sample the swisstopo swissAlti3D DEM (profile.json, sr=2056) for ascent, via a sub-metre WGS84->LV95 approximate formula (no proj4). Elevation fetched after the path draws, folded into the ascent stat; best-effort, cached, failure-tolerant. Tests + fixture added.

title: Show 'insert waypoint' affordance when hovering a path on the map
description: A user cannot tell that clicking a leg inserts a waypoint (insertOnLeg, wired at app.js:119). Add an on-hover affordance so the click target is discoverable.
design:
Preferred: Leaflet sticky tooltip bound to each polyline in setRoute() (app.js:118). pl.bindTooltip('+ Insert waypoint', {sticky:true, direction:'top', opacity:.9, className:'insert-tip'}) — sticky makes it follow the cursor along the line. Style .insert-tip via a leaflet-tooltip CSS override to match panel look (paper bg, --ink text, --line border).

Also signal clickability with the cursor: CSS rule path.leaflet-interactive{cursor:copy} targets only polylines (waypoint markers are divIcons, div.wp, not svg paths), so markers keep grab cursor.

Alternative considered: message in the router panel instead of a tooltip — rejected as farther from the action and more layout churn. Tooltip text stays generic so it reads the same for routed/direct/error legs (insertOnLeg inherits the leg's mode).
acceptance_criteria: Hovering any leg polyline shows a small 'insert waypoint' tooltip near the cursor and a copy/pointer cursor; clicking still inserts a waypoint on that leg. Waypoint markers still show the grab cursor and no tooltip.
close_reason: Implemented: bidirectional leg-list↔map hover highlight (emphasizeLeg + legLayers) and sticky 'insert waypoint' tooltip + copy cursor on route polylines.

title: Link leg list ↔ map path with bidirectional hover highlight
description: Make the connection between a leg-list row and its path on the map visible, so the user sees in advance what a Routed/Direct toggle (or remove) will affect before clicking. Hovering a leg row highlights its polyline; hovering a polyline highlights its row.
design:
Mapping: list item i (i>0) owns the INCOMING leg i (waypoint i-1 -> i), the same leg its Routed/Direct toggle controls. setRoute already tags each polyline with o.leg=i.

1. In setRoute() (app.js:112) keep a module-level Map legLayers (leg index -> polyline), cleared at the top of each setRoute, and legLayers.set(o.leg, pl) per leg.
2. Add helper emphasizeLeg(i, on): style legLayers.get(i) (weight+2, bringToFront on hover) AND toggle class .hot on the matching li[data-leg=i]. Single code path so both directions share highlight logic.
3. renderLegList() (app.js:130): set li.dataset.leg=i; for i>0 attach mouseenter/mouseleave -> emphasizeLeg(i, true/false). Start row (i=0) has no incoming leg -> no highlight.
4. Replace the existing per-polyline mouseover/mouseout weight tweak (app.js:120-121) with emphasizeLeg(o.leg,...) so map->list lights the row too.
5. CSS: ol.legs li.hot{background:var(--paper)} (or a subtle tint) for the row highlight.

Reconcile with recompute() async rebuild: legLayers is repopulated every setRoute, so stale refs are naturally dropped.
acceptance_criteria: Hovering a leg row thickens/raises its polyline on the map; mouse-out restores it. Hovering a polyline tints its leg row in the panel. Start row (no incoming leg) highlights nothing. Works after add/remove/toggle without stale highlights.
close_reason: Implemented: bidirectional leg-list↔map hover highlight (emphasizeLeg + legLayers) and sticky 'insert waypoint' tooltip + copy cursor on route polylines.

title: Native Swiss projection (LV95 / EPSG:2056) via proj4
description: Map currently runs in Web Mercator (EPSG:3857). Optionally render in the true Swiss grid LV95 / EPSG:2056 via proj4 for an authentic Landeskarte projection and to enable LV95-based swisstopo services (e.g. the DEM height query used by the direct-leg elevation feature).
design: Add proj4 + proj4leaflet, define EPSG:2056 with swisstopo WMTS LV95 tile matrix. Coordinate conversions (currently explicit WGS84<->3857 via Leaflet) must round-trip cleanly for waypoints, GPX export (stays WGS84), and BRouter (stays lng,lat). Shared WGS84->LV95 helper also unblocks the swissAlti3D height query.
acceptance_criteria: Base map renders in LV95; waypoints/drag/insert still land correctly; GPX and BRouter I/O remain WGS84; provides a reusable WGS84->LV95 conversion.
notes:
Nice-to-have; enables the DEM height query. Lower priority than the UX and batching work.
Deferred (2026-07-04). P3, purely visual; the WGS84->LV95 helper that was its main practical driver already shipped with strt-9mw (wgs84ToLv95 in src/app.js). Best done when the map can be visually verified in a real browser — a projection swap can't be confirmed for alignment from a headless env. Would also flip the tile-shape test from /3857/ to /2056/ and add proj4 + proj4leaflet.
